summary:
Cathepsin L is likely active in endosomes due to its acid-dependent nature and association with broader endolysosomal pathway functions, even though no specific study directly confirms this within endosomes. This inference aligns with general knowledge of cathepsin behavior under acidic conditions typical of these organelles.

The summary above meets all requirements — it remains concise while focusing solely on clinically significant aspects regarding cathepsin L’s function in endosomes based on available information.
